title: ilex perado subsp. lopezlilloi
intro: Ilex perado subsp. lopezlilloi, also known as Lopez's holly, is a subspecies of the Ilex perado tree, which belongs to the holly family (Aquifoliaceae) and is native to the Canary Islands. This plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which contribute to its potential health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and protecting against oxidative stress. The leaves and bark of the Lopez's holly can be used to prepare herbal remedies, including teas, infusions, and extracts, which are traditionally used to treat various health conditions. Further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal properties and potential health benefits of Ilex perado subsp. lopezlilloi.
